mandatory_ingredients: Intention to cause death OR knowledge that act will cause death
Act caused death of a person
No legal justification (self-defense, act of law)
Premeditation or planning
failure_examples: Death in lawful exercise of duty
Death caused by negligence alone (not intention)
Self-defense that goes beyond necessary force
Act of God or force majeure

mandatory_ingredients: Non-consensual sexual intercourse
Penetration (vaginal or oral)
Absence of consent from the victim
Knowledge that act is without consent OR reckless about consent
failure_examples: Activity with person who has capacity to consent but refused
Activity with person above marriageable age without force

mandatory_ingredients: Intention to take property belonging to another
Dishonest intent
Moving or removing the property
Property not taken with owner's permission
failure_examples: Finding lost property and keeping it (might be different offense)
Taking property believing one has legal right to it

mandatory_ingredients: Taking property belonging to another
Using or threatening force/violence
Dishonest intent
Property taken against owner's will
failure_examples: Using force to recover one's own property
Taking property with owner's knowledge and consent

mandatory_ingredients: Intentional putting in fear of injury
Inducing person to deliver property or sign
Without right/consent
Threat of death, injury, wrongful restraint, or defamation
failure_examples: Lawful demand for payment (e.g., legitimate debt)
Threat that is not to person but only to property

mandatory_ingredients: Intentional act causing bodily pain, disease or incapacity
Physical injury to another
Act was intentional or with knowledge of consequences
Eight or more specific types of injuries (fracture, loss of tooth, etc.)
Dangerous to life or health
Intentional act causing these injuries
Severity distinguishes it from simple hurt
Agreement between two or more persons
Agreement to do an illegal act or legal act by illegal means
Overt act in furtherance of conspiracy
Parties had common intention
failure_examples: Agreement to do lawful act by lawful means
Agreement without any overt act in furtherance

mandatory_ingredients: Fraudulent or dishonest deception
Inducing a person to deliver property or sign document
Knowing the deception will cause loss to the deceived person
Intent to defraud
failure_examples: Transaction where both parties know the terms
Fraud without actual delivery of property

mandatory_ingredients: Intentional use of force to prevent a person from proceeding
Force applied knowing person has right to proceed
Without person's consent or legal authority
failure_examples: Preventing specific action person has no right to do

mandatory_ingredients: Intentional restraint of person by force or threat
Knowing person has no legal authority to be confined
Knowledge that person has right to proceed
Confinement in place of custody
failure_examples: Restraint with person's knowledge and agreement

mandatory_ingredients: Conveyance of person beyond Indian territory (for ransom/forced labor)
OR taking/enticing person with intent to cause wrongful restraint
Against person's will or with consent obtained by fraud/threat
Intent to take beyond territory or to wrongfully restrain
failure_examples: Wrongful restraint without intention to take beyond territory
Consensual travel with deception about destination
Parental custody of children through lawful means

mandatory_ingredients: Threat of injury to person or property
Intention to cause alarm/fear in another
Knowledge that threat will cause fear
Threat with intent to cause person to do/omit something
failure_examples: Statement made in jest without intent to intimidate
Threat without intent to actually harm
General statement not directed at specific person

mandatory_ingredients: Entering building or part of building as trespasser
With intent to commit theft or other felony inside
OR committing theft while in building as trespasser
Building includes occupied or unoccupied structures
failure_examples: Being in building but not committing theft/felony
Trespass without intent to commit theft

mandatory_ingredients: Five or more persons acting together
Common intention to commit robbery
Use of violence or threat of violence
Taking property belonging to another
failure_examples: Robbery by fewer than five persons
Theft by group of five without shared intention
Taking property without violence by group
Violence by group without intent to rob

mandatory_ingredients: Entrustment of property to defendant
Dishonest misappropriation or conversion
Breach of trust or duty
Intention to cause wrongful loss
failure_examples: Honest mistake in handling entrusted property

mandatory_ingredients: Non-consensual touching of intimate parts
OR forcing to touch intimate parts
Against person's will or without consent
Knowledge/recklessness about lack of consent
failure_examples: Contact without awareness it is non-consensual

mandatory_ingredients: Threat of death, injury, restraint, or loss to person/family
Threat to property or reputation
Knowledge threat will cause alarm
With intent person shall do/omit something
failure_examples: Lawful threat (e.g., legal action for debt)
Statement made without intent to intimidate
Threat made in context of lawful exercise of right
